calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate From The Suspension Of Petitioners Driving Privileges.
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, E. Michael Linscheid of the law offices of E. Michael Linscheid (510-451-2010) appears as counsel for petitioner

Deputy Attorney General, William J. McMahon of the California Department of Justice (510-879-0297) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER CLAYTON KOPP's Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate From The Suspension Of Petitioners Driving Privileges is DENIED. Per the independent judgment standard of review, the weight of the evidence supports the decision of the hearing officer that Mr. Kopp impermissibly refused the request of CHP Officer Nathaniel Woodall that Mr. Kopp submit to a blood test after Officer Woodall determined that there were no available evidentiary breath machines that he or anyone available to him could properly operate. The testimony of Officer Woodall adequately supports all of the foregoing findings and the calibration logs do not show otherwise. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR No. 9711, e-mail: lreding@sftc.org. (L&M302)
